message:
test(harness): add 21 real-backend composition tests (bd-mblr.2.1.1)

Exercises pager/WAL/MVCC/B-tree over file-backed VFS paths: persistence
round-trips, transactions, rollback, WAL mode, B-tree splits, overflow
pages, concurrent mode, multi-table atomicity, indexes, type diversity,
NULL handling, and deterministic seed workloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -78,8 +78,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.2","title":"Track 2: Differential Corpus Expansion and Gap Harvesting","description":"## Context
40% parity means we are under-sampling behavior space. Corpus depth and failure localization must increase.

## Goal
Build a high-power differential corpus engine: upstream ingestion, metamorphic generation, shrink/minimize, and real-db replay.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Corpus breadth and depth materially expanded across SQL/storage/extensions.
- Every mismatch emits normalized signature + minimized repro.
- Replay is deterministic and drift-aware.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:21.979284569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:48:26.248471780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","differential","parity100"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:21.979284569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:12.535454393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1371,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 2 operating notes:
- Purpose is to increase behavioral sample power so parity gaps are discovered quickly and localized cleanly.
- Output of this track is not only more cases, but better failure compression and drift observability.

Design choices:
- Upstream corpus ingestion is normalized into Track 1 taxonomy buckets.
- Metamorphic transforms require soundness sketch before activation.
- Mismatch minimization should produce smallest reproducible SQL/workload plus canonical signature.
- Real-db replay must classify regime shifts (stable vs drift) and preserve deterministic envelopes.

Test/logging requirements:
- Unit tests for transform soundness and minimizer correctness.
- E2E replay tests with fixed seeds and deterministic artifact IDs.
- Logs capture seed, transform chain, minimized repro, signature class, and subsystem probability hints.

Definition of done:
- Gap discovery throughput rises, duplicate mismatch volume drops, nightly drift reports generated.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:24Z"}]}

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:25Z"},{"id":1441,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.2.3 — Mismatch Minimizer and Canonical Signature Pipeline

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/mismatch_minimizer.rs` — automatic mismatch minimizer,
canonical signature extraction, and deduplication pipeline for differential test failures.

### Key Components

1. **`Subsystem` enum** — 12 subsystem variants (Parser, Resolver, Planner, Vdbe, Storage,
   Wal, Mvcc, Functions, Extension, TypeSystem, Pragma, Unknown) with Display impl.

2. **`MismatchSignature`** — Content-addressed fingerprint (truncated SHA-256, 16 hex chars)
   incorporating classification, subsystem, schema, and minimal workload. Enables deduplication
   of repeated failures to same root cause.

3. **`MinimalReproduction`** — Complete reproduction artifact with schema, minimal workload,
   reduction ratio, divergence details, and reproduction command. JSON serializable.

4. **`minimize_workload()`** — Delta-debugging minimizer:
   - Phase 1: Binary partition reduction (doubling granularity)
   - Phase 2: 1-minimal reduction (try removing each statement individually)
   - Configurable iteration limits and workload size caps
   - Schema statements preserved (never removed)

5. **`attribute_subsystem()`** — Heuristic-based subsystem attribution:
   - Window functions (OVER clause) checked before general functions
   - Extension detection (JSON, FTS, R-tree)
   - Function, PRAGMA, type system, WAL/storage, planner, VDBE attribution

6. **`deduplicate()`** — Groups reproductions by signature hash, sorts by triage priority,
   reports duplicate counts.

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 19 tests, all passing:
```
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_signature_deterministic ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_signature_differs_on_classification ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_signature_differs_on_subsystem ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_signature_display ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_json_to_extension ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_pragma_to_pragma ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_window_to_vdbe ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_join_to_planner ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_function_to_functions ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_attribute_cast_to_type_system ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimize_single_statement ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimize_removes_non_contributing ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimize_preserves_required_statements ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimize_empty_workload ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimize_no_reproduction ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_deduplicate_identical_signatures ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_deduplicate_different_signatures ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_reproduction_json_roundtrip ... ok
test mismatch_minimizer::tests::test_minimizer_config_defaults ... ok
```

**Reproduction**: `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib mismatch_minimizer`

**Quality gates**: cargo check clean, cargo fmt clean

### Files Changed
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/mismatch_minimizer.rs` (new, ~940 lines)
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs` (added `pub mod mismatch_minimizer;`)
","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:15:42Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1dp9.3","title":"Track 3: SQL Compiler and Runtime Semantic Closure","description":"## Context
Core semantic mismatches live in parser/resolver/planner/VDBE/PRAGMA/error surfaces.

## Goal
Close SQL pipeline behavior gaps end-to-end with oracle-backed tests and detailed diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Open semantic gaps mapped and eliminated.
- Unit and differential tests cover each closed gap.
- Error code/message parity verified for supported surface.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.323219840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:55.813184326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity100","planner","sql","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.323219840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:12.860624722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:13.189226700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1372,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 3 operating notes:
- This is SQL semantic parity closure from parser through execution.
- Every mismatch must map to a precise semantic contract and code location.

Design choices:
- Gap map should prioritize by expected user impact and closure leverage.
- Parser/resolver/PRAGMA and planner/VDBE closures are separated for clean blast-radius control.
- Error-code/message parity is part of behavior contract where surface is declared supported.

Test/logging requirements:
- Unit tests per semantic clause closed.
- Differential E2E scripts for each closure wave.
- Detailed divergence logging: SQL text, normalized AST/plan/opcode trace, output diff, first divergence stage.

Definition of done:
- No open Track 3 mismatches on declared SQL surface and no undocumented divergences.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:24Z"}]}

Output: explicit gate policy with hard fail criteria and temporary exceptions protocol.","design":"Represent thresholds and exceptions as structured config so CI jobs can enforce without custom parsing.","acceptance_criteria":"- Threshold table exists for baseline and target phases, with clear promotion criteria between phases.
- No-mock critical-path evidence requirement is machine-checkable.
- Temporary exceptions require explicit owner, justification, and sunset date.","notes":"Do not encode policy in scattered scripts; keep a single authoritative source consumed by gate jobs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:52.025549254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:04:36.844514607Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:04:36.844494780Z","close_reason":"Created docs/coverage-slo-policy.md with thresholds (70% line hard fail, 80% target), per-crate requirements, realism tier requirements for critical paths, staged rollout plan, and exception protocol.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:38:31.086244562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:38:31.344794465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:38:31.593312092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:37:52.025549254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-mblr.2.1.2","title":"Conflict/recovery assertions on integrated backend","description":"Add integrated tests that force page-level conflicts, rollback/retry paths, and recovery invariants on real backend components. Output: high-signal assertions proving correct conflict semantics end-to-end.","design":"Model conflict scenarios as declarative sequences (actor, page set, operation, expected visibility) to maximize reproducibility.","acceptance_criteria":"- Tests induce page-level conflicts and validate retry/rollback/recovery correctness with deterministic outcomes.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.2.2.1","title":"File-backed connection lifecycle matrix tests","description":"Implement lifecycle matrix over real database files: create/open/close/reopen/begin/savepoint/commit/rollback/checkpoint permutations.  {"id":"bd-mblr.7.1.2","title":"Differential runner with mismatch reducer","description":"Build execution runner comparing Apollo vs SQLite outputs/plans and add deterministic shrinking to minimal failing workloads.","design":"Implement a differential runner that executes paired workloads, normalizes outputs, and shrinks mismatches into minimal reproducible traces.","acceptance_criteria":"- Runner supports deterministic replay from seed with full transcript export and normalized outputs.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.1.3","title":"Differential CI lane and evidence artifacts","description":"Integrate differential suite into CI with reproducible artifacts, replay commands, and structured logs for each mismatch class.","design":"Create a CI lane for differential validation with bounded tiers, artifact retention policy, and structured summaries for trend analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"- Mainline CI runs deterministic smoke differential suites and scheduled jobs run expanded suites.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.2","title":"Long-Run Concurrency Endurance and History Checking","description":"Add multi-hour/day concurrent-writer endurance workloads with history/invariant validation to catch temporal correctness and starvation issues not visible in short scenarios.","design":"Establish long duration concurrent workloads with invariant probes so reliability defects that only appear under sustained pressure become reproducible and diagnosable.","acceptance_criteria":"- Soak profiles represent realistic contention classes across tables, indexes, and transaction sizes.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.2.2","title":"Concurrent soak executor with periodic invariant probes","description":"Implement deterministic soak harness with checkpoints, invariant probes, and controlled fault injections across long durations.","design":"Implement a soak executor that runs concurrent actors for long durations while emitting periodic checkpoints and invariant probe results.","acceptance_criteria":"- Executor supports configurable duration, actor counts, workload mixes, and probe cadence.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.2.3","title":"Soak triage and replay workflow","description":"Create failure summarization, artifact packaging, and deterministic replay/minimization path for long-run soak anomalies.","design":"Provide triage and replay tooling that ingests soak artifacts, reconstructs timeline state, and reproduces failures in minimized form.","acceptance_criteria":"- Triage workflow identifies first failing invariant window and links to relevant actor transcripts.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.3","title":"Performance Regression Governance","description":"Establish workload-based performance baselines and automated regression detection with explicit SLO policy and reviewable waivers.","design":"Define performance governance with stable benchmark corpus, noise aware baselines, and clear policy for blocking regressions versus waived exceptions.","acceptance_criteria":"- Benchmark corpus covers throughput, latency distribution, contention behavior, and recovery costs.
